name: Baked Prawns and Mungbean Noodles- Kung Op Wun Sen
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Mungbean noodles, soaked and cut into short lengths
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the oil in a wok, heat and stir fry the coriander root, ginger, pepper, and onion until fragrant.
2. Remove from the wok and place in a mixing bowl.
3. Add the noodles, sauces (Maggi sauce), salt, sugar, sesame oil, and whiskey to the bowl. Toss the noodles until well coated.
4. Divide the mixture into four individual portions. Place each portion in a lidded cup and close the lids.
5. Arrange the cups on a baking tray and bake at 460°F (240°C) for about 10 minutes or until the prawns are done.
6. Serve hot with fresh vegetables such as tomatoes and spring onions.
From "The Elegant Taste of Thailand, Cha Am Cuisine" by Sisamon Kongpan and Pinyo Srisawat. SLG Books, Berkeley and Hong Kong, 1989. ISBN 0-943389-05-4.
